codeReview.summary: The feature improves delay defaults and adds useful governance tests, but it still misses part of the deployment contract and introduces a security regression in the governed entrypoint. The system is still bootstrapped by deploying AccessManager and protocol contracts before the Governor stack exists, and the deploy script lowers target admin delays from the configured 1 hour to 60 seconds.

issues.description: `deployGovernedSystem()` still delegates to `deployDualVmSystem()` before deploying GovernanceToken, TimelockController, or DualVMGovernor. Because `deployDualVmSystem()` creates the AccessManager, deploys the market contracts, deploys MarketVersionRegistry, and even registers/activates v1 first (`/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/deploySystem.ts:72-110`), the implemented flow is still EOA-bootstrap-first rather than the required `Governor -> TimelockController -> AccessManager -> protocol contracts` chain described in the feature contract and `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/AGENTS.md`. The added tests only verify the final post-handoff state, so this contract miss is currently undetected.

The governed deployment entrypoint now forces `adminDelaySeconds` to `LIVE_ROLE_EXECUTION_DELAYS_SECONDS.riskAdmin` (60) when `ADMIN_DELAY_SECONDS` is unset. That silently overrides the helper's normal default of `TARGET_ADMIN_DELAY_SECONDS` = 3600 seconds (`/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/config/marketConfig.ts:21`, `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/deploySystem.ts:65`) and weakens the admin-delay protection on all governed deployments by 59 minutes. The feature asked for 60-second role execution delays, not for shortening the target admin delay.

The helper returns `...base` unchanged, so `deployment.governance.admin` still reports the original deployer from `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/deploySystem.ts:191-198` even after the timelock is granted AccessManager admin and the deployer is revoked. `scripts/deployGoverned.ts` works around this by patching the manifest manually at lines 52-55, but any other caller of `deployGovernedSystem()` receives stale governance metadata.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Solidity worker skill's mandatory 'Write tests FIRST (red)' step does not fit deployment-helper refinement work very well. This worker had to inspect and adjust an existing helper first, and the handoff explicitly called out that the prescribed red-first flow was unnatural for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md:24-33` requires a failing-test-first workflow, while `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-16T21-42-39-998Z__governor-deployment-helpers__c5e69e9f-e8f8-49c0-bdb8-1ceb97dc97fb.json:56-60` records the worker's suggestion that reading/modifying the existing deployment helper first was more natural here.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and the governed deployment/test files. Result: fail, because the helper still does not implement the required Governor-first deployment flow and the governed deploy script weakens admin-delay security by defaulting to 60 seconds instead of the configured 1 hour.
